behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the vision model’s real classification judgment on explicit content (LLM-DEP). A stubbed classifier can only verify routing after a flag, not whether explicit images are actually flagged. Rejected Workflow: behavior text covers a single model decision path (image classification) plus deterministic hold/publish handling, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real explicit test image through the moderation endpoint with the production-equivalent vision model enabled; assert the resulting moderation state is 'held for human review' and that no publish event is emitted.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This behavior is explicitly about robustness of model judgment under obfuscation, which depends on real AI text-model output quality (LLM-DEP). Deterministic mocks hide the core failure. Rejected Workflow: only one agentic classifier decision is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants against the real text moderation model via the service and assert each is flagged/held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is orchestration/state handoff across local modules (image result + text result -> final publish decision). This can be caught by wiring real service modules with deterministic pass outcomes from moderation adapters. Rejected Agentic: behavior text says 'passes both content checks' and does not require validating model judgment quality. Rejected Workflow: no requirement to validate multiple real model decisions, only downstream decisioning when both are pass.
behaviors.test_description: In an in-process integration test, wire moderation pipeline modules with deterministic 'pass' responses for image and text checks, submit a post, and assert publish is emitted and neither hold nor drop paths are taken.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end timing for a multi-stage pipeline that includes two AI model steps in sequence (MULTI-STEP + LLM-involved stages). Measuring true 5s completion risk requires real pipeline execution, including real model-call behavior and cross-stage handoff overhead. Rejected System: behavior text requires the full pipeline with both AI checks, which is a multi-agentic-step boundary rather than a single external-infra interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end performance test with real moderation pipeline stages (image model call, text model call, final decision path) and measure submission-to-decision latency; assert p95 (or required percentile) is <= 5 seconds during peak-like load.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: This is a failure-handling contract between moderation adapter and queue/decision modules. A deterministic unavailable/error response from the vision client is sufficient to reproduce and catch the bug. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network/OS failure semantics, only correct retry queue behavior on unavailability. Rejected Agentic: no dependence on model output content, only outage path handling.
behaviors.test_description: Run an integration test where the vision-model adapter returns an 'unavailable' error; submit an image post and assert it is enqueued for retry and explicitly not marked approved/published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency for a borderline image depends on real vision-model behavior under repeated calls (LLM-DEP); mocks can force consistency and miss genuine instability. Rejected Workflow: although submissions repeat over time, each check is the same single model decision type; behavior text does not require a multi-agentic pipeline across different decision stages.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the defined short window using the real vision moderation path and assert decision consistency rate meets the requirement (or strict equality if specified).
